Name: traffic-ticket-contest
Description: A universally-recognizable case-management domain: a driver receives a traffic citation, may pay it or contest it at a hearing, and faces payment penalties and license-point consequences. Multi-jurisdiction: deadlines, fines, point caps, and suspension thresholds are first-class data, not hardcoded. Four state machines (Citation lifecycle, Contest/Hearing, Payment/Penalty, License-points) are modeled as CALCULATED status fields on the Citation, derived from raw facts + child events + jurisdiction rules, with an append-only CaseEvents log mirroring each transition.

schema.name: JurisdictionId
schema.Description: Surrogate key for the jurisdiction (off-screen).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the jurisdiction code (e.g. 'CA-LA' -> 'ca-la').

schema.name: Code
schema.Description: Short human code for the jurisdiction, e.g. 'CA-LA', 'NY-NYC', 'TX-AUS'.

schema.name: DisplayName
schema.Description: Full readable jurisdiction name, e.g. 'Los Angeles County, California'.

schema.name: DaysToRespond
schema.Description: Number of days after issuance the driver has to respond (pay or request a hearing) before default judgment.

schema.name: DaysToPayAfterRuling
schema.Description: Number of days after a guilty ruling (or after issuance if not contested) the fine is due before it is late.

schema.name: LatePenaltyPct
schema.Description: Percentage surcharge added to the assessed amount once a payment is late (e.g. 0.25 = 25%).

schema.name: DaysLateToCollections
schema.Description: Number of days a payment may remain late before it is referred to collections.

schema.name: PointSuspensionThreshold
schema.Description: Total active license points at or above which a driver's license is suspended in this jurisdiction.

schema.name: PointWarningThreshold
schema.Description: Total active license points at or above which a driver receives an advisory warning (below suspension).

schema.name: TrafficSchoolPointCap
schema.Description: Maximum violation points for which traffic school is offered as a points-reducing option in this jurisdiction.

schema.name: ViolationTypes
schema.Description: Reverse FK: violation types governed by this jurisdiction.

schema.name: Citations
schema.Description: Reverse FK: citations issued under this jurisdiction.

schema.name: CountOfCitations
schema.Description: Number of citations issued under this jurisdiction.

schema.name: ViolationTypeId
schema.Description: Surrogate key for the violation type (off-screen).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the violation code (e.g. 'CVC-22350' -> 'cvc-22350').

schema.name: Code
schema.Description: Statute/vehicle-code reference for the violation, e.g. 'CVC-22350'.

schema.name: Jurisdiction
schema.Description: FK to the Jurisdiction whose rules govern this violation type.

schema.name: JurisdictionLabel
schema.Description: Display name of the governing jurisdiction.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Plain-English description of the violation, e.g. 'Unsafe speed for conditions'.

schema.name: BaseFineUsd
schema.Description: Base fine amount in USD for this violation before any penalties or reductions.

schema.name: Points
schema.Description: License points assessed for a conviction of this violation.

schema.name: IsSchoolEligibleByCap
schema.Description: Whether this violation's points fall at or below the jurisdiction's traffic-school point cap (jurisdiction rule applied to the violation).

schema.name: Citations
schema.Description: Reverse FK: citations issued for this violation type.

schema.name: CountOfCitations
schema.Description: Number of citations issued for this violation type.

data.Description: Unsafe speed for conditions (basic speed law)
Failure to stop at a red signal
Handheld cell phone use while driving
Failure to obey a steady red signal

schema.name: DriverId
schema.Description: Surrogate key for the driver (off-screen).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the license number (e.g. 'D1234567' -> 'd1234567').

schema.name: LicenseNumber
schema.Description: Driver's license number; the natural identifier for the driver.

schema.name: FirstName
schema.Description: Driver's first name.

schema.name: LastName
schema.Description: Driver's last name.

schema.name: FullName
schema.Description: Display name, last-comma-first.

schema.name: HomeJurisdiction
schema.Description: FK to the Jurisdiction that issued the driver's license (governs point/suspension rules for the driver).

schema.name: HomeJurisdictionLabel
schema.Description: Display name of the driver's home jurisdiction.

schema.name: SuspensionThreshold
schema.Description: Point-suspension threshold pulled from the driver's home jurisdiction.

schema.name: WarningThreshold
schema.Description: Point-warning threshold pulled from the driver's home jurisdiction.

schema.name: Citations
schema.Description: Reverse FK: citations issued to this driver.

schema.name: CountOfCitations
schema.Description: Number of citations issued to this driver.

schema.name: ActivePoints
schema.Description: Sum of license points across this driver's citations that resulted in a conviction (guilty/unpaid-default) and are still point-active.

schema.name: LicenseStatus
schema.Description: License-points state machine for the driver: Suspended at/above the suspension threshold, Warning at/above the warning threshold, otherwise Valid.

schema.name: CitationId
schema.Description: Surrogate key for the citation (off-screen).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the citation number (e.g. 'TC-2026-0001' -> 'tc-2026-0001').

schema.name: CitationNumber
schema.Description: Human citation/ticket number printed on the ticket.

schema.name: Driver
schema.Description: FK to the cited Driver.

schema.name: DriverLabel
schema.Description: Display name of the cited driver.

schema.name: ViolationType
schema.Description: FK to the cited ViolationType.

schema.name: ViolationLabel
schema.Description: Description of the cited violation.

schema.name: Jurisdiction
schema.Description: FK to the Jurisdiction in which the citation was issued (governs deadlines, penalties, points).

schema.name: JurisdictionLabel
schema.Description: Display name of the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: BaseFineUsd
schema.Description: Base fine for the cited violation, pulled from the ViolationType.

schema.name: ViolationPoints
schema.Description: License points for the cited violation, pulled from the ViolationType.

schema.name: IssuedOn
schema.Description: Date the citation was issued to the driver.

schema.name: AsOfDate
schema.Description: The 'today' reference date used to evaluate deadlines for this citation (lets seed data exercise overdue/late states deterministically).

schema.name: RespondedOn
schema.Description: Date the driver responded (paid or requested a hearing). Null if no response yet.

schema.name: ContestRequested
schema.Description: Whether the driver elected to contest the citation (request a hearing) rather than pay.

schema.name: PaidOn
schema.Description: Date the fine was paid in full. Null if not yet paid.

schema.name: AmountPaidUsd
schema.Description: Total amount the driver has paid against this citation. Null/0 if nothing paid.

schema.name: DaysToRespond
schema.Description: Response window in days, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: DaysToPayAfterRuling
schema.Description: Days-to-pay window, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: LatePenaltyPct
schema.Description: Late-penalty percentage, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: DaysLateToCollections
schema.Description: Days-late-to-collections window, pulled from the issuing jurisdiction.

schema.name: ResponseDueDate
schema.Description: Deadline to respond: IssuedOn + the jurisdiction's response window.

schema.name: DaysUntilResponseDue
schema.Description: Days remaining (negative if overdue) until the response deadline, measured from AsOfDate.

schema.name: IsResponseOverdue
schema.Description: True when no response was filed and the response deadline has passed as of AsOfDate.

schema.name: CountOfHearings
schema.Description: Number of hearing records attached to this citation.

schema.name: LatestHearingOutcome
schema.Description: Outcome of the most recent hearing for this citation (Pending if a hearing exists but has no outcome; blank if no hearing).

schema.name: ContestStatus
schema.Description: Contest/Hearing state machine: NotContested when the driver did not elect to contest; otherwise HearingRequested -> Scheduled -> Heard, reflected from the latest hearing's outcome.

schema.name: IsDismissed
schema.Description: True when the latest hearing outcome dismissed the citation.

schema.name: IsGuilty
schema.Description: True when the driver is liable: either found guilty/upheld at hearing, or defaulted by missing the response deadline without contesting.

schema.name: AmountDueUsd
schema.Description: Amount currently owed: 0 if dismissed; otherwise the base fine plus the jurisdiction's late penalty if the payment is late.

schema.name: PaymentDueDate
schema.Description: Date the fine is due: the later anchor (response deadline) plus the jurisdiction's days-to-pay window.

schema.name: IsPaymentLate
schema.Description: True when the driver is liable, has not paid in full, and the payment due date has passed as of AsOfDate.

schema.name: IsInCollections
schema.Description: True when a late payment has remained unpaid past the jurisdiction's collections window.

schema.name: PaymentStatus
schema.Description: Payment/Penalty state machine: NotOwed (dismissed) -> Paid -> Collections -> Late -> Due. Evaluated in priority order.

schema.name: EffectivePoints
schema.Description: License points this citation actually contributes to the driver: the violation's points if the driver is liable and not dismissed, otherwise 0. Drives the driver's ActivePoints rollup.

schema.name: CitationStatus
schema.Description: Citation lifecycle state machine: Issued -> Responded -> InContest -> Adjudicated -> Closed. The top-level status synthesizing the other tracks.

schema.name: Hearings
schema.Description: Reverse FK: hearing records for this citation.

schema.name: Payments
schema.Description: Reverse FK: payment records for this citation.

schema.name: CaseEvents
schema.Description: Reverse FK: append-only case-event log entries for this citation.

schema.name: HearingId
schema.Description: Surrogate key for the hearing (off-screen).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the hearing reference number.

schema.name: HearingNumber
schema.Description: Human hearing reference number.

schema.name: Citation
schema.Description: FK to the Citation being contested at this hearing.

schema.name: CitationLabel
schema.Description: Citation number this hearing concerns.

schema.name: RequestedOn
schema.Description: Date the hearing was requested by the driver.

schema.name: ScheduledFor
schema.Description: Date the hearing is scheduled for. Null if requested but not yet scheduled.

schema.name: Outcome
schema.Description: Outcome of the hearing: 'Pending' (scheduled, not yet heard), 'Dismissed', 'Guilty', 'Upheld', or 'Reduced'. Null if not yet scheduled.

schema.name: Notes
schema.Description: Free-text notes from the hearing.

data.Notes: Driver contests red-light timing; awaiting hearing.
Radar calibration records not produced; dismissed.

schema.name: PaymentId
schema.Description: Surrogate key for the payment (off-screen).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the payment reference number.

schema.name: PaymentNumber
schema.Description: Human payment reference number.

schema.name: Citation
schema.Description: FK to the Citation this payment is applied to.

schema.name: CitationLabel
schema.Description: Citation number this payment concerns.

schema.name: PaidOn
schema.Description: Date the payment was made.

schema.name: AmountUsd
schema.Description: Amount of the payment in USD.

schema.name: Method
schema.Description: Payment method, e.g. 'card', 'check', 'cash', 'online'.

schema.name: CaseEventId
schema.Description: Surrogate key for the case event (off-screen).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Kebab-cased PK derived from the event reference number.

schema.name: EventNumber
schema.Description: Human event reference number (sortable, e.g. 'EV-2026-0001').

schema.name: Citation
schema.Description: FK to the Citation this event belongs to.

schema.name: CitationLabel
schema.Description: Citation number this event concerns.

schema.name: OccurredOn
schema.Description: Date the event occurred.

schema.name: Track
schema.Description: Which state machine this transition belongs to: 'Citation', 'Contest', 'Payment', or 'License'.

schema.name: FromState
schema.Description: Prior state on the track (null for the initial event).

schema.name: ToState
schema.Description: New state on the track after this transition.

schema.name: Note
schema.Description: Free-text note describing the transition.

schema.name: MetaKey
schema.Description: Metadata key (logical PK).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Mirrors MetaKey as the row's Name.

schema.name: ValueType
schema.Description: One of 'string', 'object', or 'array'.

schema.name: StringValue
schema.Description: Scalar string value when ValueType is 'string'.

schema.name: JsonValue
schema.Description: JSON-encoded value when ValueType is 'object' or 'array'.

data.StringValue: A traffic ticket everyone understands, modeled as four state machines and a multi-jurisdiction rules engine.

A driver gets a citation. They can pay it or contest it at a hearing. Miss the deadline and you default; lose the hearing and you owe; pay late and a jurisdiction-specific penalty applies; rack up points and your license is warned then suspended. Every deadline, fine, penalty, point cap, and threshold is a row in the Jurisdictions table — change a jurisdiction's rule and every citation under it re-derives. The four state machines (Citation, Contest, Payment, License) are calculated fields, never stored-and-mutated.

data.JsonValue: ["Show how a familiar, deadline-driven case flow decomposes into calculated state machines","Demonstrate a multi-jurisdiction rules engine where regulations are data, not code","Illustrate an append-only event log coexisting with computed current state"]

["Citation: Issued -> Responded -> InContest -> Adjudicated -> Closed","Contest: NotContested | HearingRequested -> Scheduled -> Heard","Payment: Pending -> Due -> Late -> Collections | Paid | NotOwed","License: Valid -> Warning -> Suspended"]
